title: Triage - Standard First Aid (SFA) Comprehensive Quiz

questions.question: From which language does the word 'triage' originate?
What is the primary objective of triage?
options: To treat all patients equally regardless of condition
To quickly identify victims needing immediate stabilization or transport
To provide definitive care on scene to all casualties
To transport all patients to the nearest hospital

questions.question: The three core clinical bases of triage are _____, _____, and _____.
What does MCI stand for?
According to WHO, mass casualty incidents are characterized by:
options: Quantity, severity, and diversity of patients

questions.question: Which triage tag color indicates the highest priority (Immediate) patients?
Which tag is assigned to patients with minor injuries who are able to walk?
For a YELLOW (Delayed) tagged patient, how long may treatment and transport be delayed?
The BLACK tag is for patients who are _____ or _____, and they should not be moved to the collection point.
What is the hardest triage priority to deal with emotionally?
Which special tag is used for patients with potential or real exposure to environmental, chemical, or biological contaminants?
What is recommended for contaminated patients after they are adequately decontaminated?
options: They follow START triage as usual
They are sent directly to the hospital

questions.question: Some recommend _____ tagging for contaminated patients, using blue and the standard START color.
At which on-site triage location does 'reverse triage' occur?
At which location are victims identified for transport to the operating room or respective hospital services?
What is the name of the temporary medical treatment area established near the scene of a disaster?
Which of the following is NOT one of the four primary global pre-hospital triage systems?
In the START algorithm, what does the 'R' in RPM stand for?
START uses RPM: R for _____, P for _____, and M for _____.
In START, if a patient has respirations greater than 30 per minute, what tag is assigned?
If a patient is not breathing after opening the airway with the Head Tilt method, what START tag is assigned?
In START, how many seconds should be spent checking the radial pulse for perfusion?
In START, if a patient has respirations <30, a strong radial pulse, and can follow simple commands, what tag is assigned?
If a patient in START is unresponsive and cannot follow simple commands, what tag is assigned?
Which pediatric triage tool is a modification of START designed for children aged 1–8 years?
In JumpSTART, what priority is assigned to babies less than 12 months old?
What does the AVPU assessment used in JumpSTART stand for?
The SALT triage tool stands for: Sort, _____, _____, and Treatment/Transport.
The three categories of psychological and disaster exposure effects on children are _____, _____, and _____.
Which of the following is a pediatric MCI triage recommendation from FEMA (2014)?
options: Transport all children to the nearest hospital
Group children together in the appropriate triage areas
Separate children from their parents at all times
Only use adult triage tools for children
